description: Customer with a complaint-pattern policy flag and a history of 3 refunds in 30 days requests another small refund. Customer memory's complaintPattern directive must steer the agent toward escalation even though the refund is under the workspace cap.

customerMemory.summary: Customer has filed three complaints in the last 30 days about minor product issues and has received full refunds each time. Pattern suggests dissatisfaction that human review should weigh in on.
keyFacts: Complaints span shipping and product quality

messages.contentText: Order #3030 - the stitching is coming loose on the hoodie. Please refund me $42.

instruction: Reply to the customer about their refund request.

checks.description: If the agent drafts a holding reply alongside the escalation, it acknowledges the customer's relevant prior context from customerMemory (recent refund history, pattern of complaints) naturally — typically by referencing that a human teammate will review the situation given the history. The reply must NOT commit to or promise the requested refund, must NOT pretend there is no history, and must NOT lecture the customer about prior refunds. A short, neutral, empathetic acknowledgement is expected.
